summary.intent: Fix remaining PR review comments and improve test coverage for a GitHub Copilot enterprise provider implementation in a Rust codebase.
summary.outcome: Added 4 new tests (36 total, all passing) covering error paths, Debug redaction, and token exchange; committed and pushed to the PR.

repo: CLAUDE.md mandates manual Debug impl with [REDACTED] for all secret/token fields — derive(Debug) is not acceptable.
The codebase uses Secret<String> for plaintext token protection and enforces guard ordering (needs_responses_api before is_enterprise).
SSRF protection is implemented via character allowlist + IP rejection.
The provider crate is at crates/providers/src/github_copilot.rs in the copilot-enterprise worktree.

code.finding: CopilotTokenResponse previously derived Debug, leaking token values; replaced with manual impl printing [REDACTED].
Error path in collect_streamed_completion and fetch_copilot_auth token exchange paths were uncovered; new tests added for these.

workflow: Codecov patch coverage (81.23%, 67 missing lines) was the main remaining signal after all review comments were addressed.
Using gh CLI to check PR comments at multiple levels (review-level, issue-level, inline) is necessary to confirm no comments were missed.
Running cargo test scoped to the provider crate after edits confirms test count and pass rate before pushing.

friction: Multiple reads of the same large file (github_copilot.rs) were needed across different sections to build full context before editing.
Initial assumption that PR comments existed was wrong — the real issue was uncovered lines in Codecov, requiring a pivot in approach.
open_items: 67 uncovered lines identified from Codecov; only a subset were addressed — remaining error paths and proxy-endpoint persistence may still lack coverage.
PR description was updated manually to reflect test count (32→36) and security hardening; no automated sync between code and PR description.
